regime_justification: USDJPY hovering near 159.3, 18 pips below MOF watch-line; Katayama issued intervention warning on May 29; spread intact at 177 bps but pace compressed after ceasefire headlines; JGB 10Y at 2.65% (two-week low); vol still subdued; BOJ June meeting 6/13-14 with rising hike probability; mixed signals warrant watchful yellow.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY drifting near 160 with MOF verbal intervention, JGB yields at 2.65% (up 82% YoY), BOJ rhetoric shifting hawkish (Himino committed to further hikes), ¥/bbl easing on Iran ceasefire but still elevated, spread at 177 bps—classic Stage 1 markers.

1w.label: Unlikely
1w.driver: USDJPY pace collapsed -1.73% on ceasefire optimism; MOVE 70, VIX 15.3 (complacent); BOJ meeting 2 weeks out; no catalyst inside 7 days; spread stable 177 bps.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: BOJ meeting June 13-14 with rising hike odds; if Iran deal collapses, oil/¥ pressure returns; USDJPY sitting 18 pips below 160; applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Post-BOJ decision window; if hike delivered, carry unwind risk elevates; JGB 10Y at 2.65% already pricing some tightening; oil ceasefire fragile; scenario-weighted caution.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If BOJ holds/Iran deal firms, pressure dissipates by late June; if hike occurs, initial volatility likely absorbed within 3 weeks; tail risk lower by week 4.

bottom_line: Temporary reprieve. USDJPY eased to 159.3 on tentative US-Iran ceasefire (oil -17% in May), but MOF warned intervention remains on table and BOJ June 13-14 meeting looms with hike odds rising. Spread still wide at 177 bps, vol compressed (MOVE 70, VIX 15.3), but JGB 10Y at 2.65% (2-week low, still +82% YoY) and Himino's hawkish commitment keep carry fragile. Grade 2-week C (applied unwind prior) for BOJ catalyst; 1-week D on calm tape. Watch BOJ June decision, Iran deal stability, and USDJPY behavior around 160.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl at 13,914 (down 3.4% 1d); easing on ceasefire MOU but deal not finalized, Trump hasn't approved; if talks collapse, oil spike returns, ¥/bbl pressure reignites quickly

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY eased to 159.27 (-1.73% 5d) on reports US-Iran agreed to 60-day ceasefire extension and potential Hormuz reopening, driving WTI down 17% in May to $87.36. MOF Katayama on May 29 reiterated intervention readiness 'against excessive volatility or speculative trading,' keeping 160 as soft red line. No stage change from yesterday's yellow/Stage 1, but tape is quieter post-ceasefire headlines. JGB 10Y fell to 2.65% (2-week low), US 10Y to 4.44%, widening spread to 177 bps. Nikkei rallied +2.53% to 66,329 on risk-on rotation; VIX dropped -2.67% to 15.3. Ceasefire not finalized (Trump hasn't approved), so geopolitical tail risk persists.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 177 bps (carry still wide); USDJPY 1M vol ~7.7% (subdued, half April peak); carry-to-vol proxy ~23x (elevated—carry attractive if vol stays low but vulnerable to spikes). USDJPY pace -1.73% 5d (MOF historically reacts to pace; sharp drop reduces near-term intervention urgency). Distance from 60d mean: compressed (pair was stretched above 160 earlier in May, now back toward mean). ¥/bbl at ¥13,914 (down 3.4% on oil drop). Stage 1 (Pressure Building) justified: ¥/bbl easing but still elevated YoY, BOJ rhetoric shifting (Himino committed to 'further hikes,' April minutes hawkish), JGB up 82% YoY, USDJPY still near watch-line. 1-week grade D: ceasefire optimism, pace collapse, no catalyst inside 7 days, vol low. 2-week grade C: BOJ June 13-14 meeting with rising hike odds; if Iran deal falters, oil/¥ pressure returns; **applied unwind prior—would otherwise grade D** given benign near-term setup, but BOJ catalyst and fragile ceasefire warrant watchful C. 3-week C: post-BOJ decision window, scenario-weighted. 4-week D: if BOJ holds or hikes absorbed, pressure dissipates.
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity: BTC consolidating $70-76k range, down ~5% in 7 days despite equity strength (S&P near ATH). Positioning cautious—institutional flows muted, ETF outflows noted, open interest flat. BTC-DXY correlation weakening (DXY +0.03% 1d, BTC +0.5%—decoupling). XRP at $1.34, down ~1.2% in 7d, underperforming BTC. Risk-on equity rotation (Nikkei +2.53%, Nasdaq +0.2%) not lifting crypto—suggests liquidity sidelined, not flowing into alts. For crypto positioning: BTC holding $73k support but lacks catalyst to break $76k resistance; if carry unwind triggers (BOJ hike, intervention, vol spike), BTC likely drains to $68-70k on deleveraging (echoes Aug 2024 pattern). Current setup: wait-and-see, manage size, watch BOJ June 13-14 and USDJPY 160 retest as key inflection points.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 is benign (ceasefire optimism, pace collapse, no scheduled catalysts, vol low → D). Week 2 elevates to C because BOJ June 13-14 meeting sits inside that window with rising hike odds (Himino hawkish, April minutes showed split, core CPI accelerated). If Iran ceasefire collapses before meeting, oil/¥ pressure returns quickly. Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker given ambiguous BOJ guidance and fragile geopolitical backdrop.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 ends with BOJ decision (June 13-14); Week 3 is post-decision digestion. If BOJ hikes (even 15 bps), carry unwind risk elevates sharply (JGB 10Y already at 2.65%, spread compression likely, USDJPY could test 155). If BOJ holds, relief rally but underlying pressure (high JGB yields, MOF on edge) persists. Scenario-weighted C for both weeks given binary BOJ outcome and oil ceasefire fragility.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 C (post-BOJ volatility) steps down to Week 4 D under base case: if BOJ hikes, initial shock absorbed within 2-3 weeks (precedent: March 2024 hike caused 3-day spike, then stabilized); if BOJ holds and Iran deal firms, pressure dissipates by late June. However, if BOJ surprises dovish AND ceasefire collapses, Week 4 could re-escalate—tail risk acknowledged but lower probability by then.

watch_list: BOJ June 13-14 MPM decision and Ueda press conference tone (hike probability rising; 15 bps move would compress spread, trigger carry unwind)
USDJPY retest of 160 and MOF/Katayama response (verbal intervention 5/29 sets stage; rate-checks likely if pair probes 160 again)
US-Iran ceasefire finalization and Trump approval (MOU reported but not confirmed; collapse would spike oil, reverse ¥/bbl relief, re-stress Japan)
JGB 10Y behavior around 2.7% (pulled back to 2.65% on ceasefire; if rallies back to 2.8% on fiscal/BOJ concerns, signals renewed carry stress)
Cross-asset vol (MOVE, VIX) reaction to BOJ meeting (currently 70/15.3—complacent; BOJ surprise could spike both, trigger broader risk-off)
